title: What is i-mode?
body: ![i-mode logo](http://farm3.static.flickr.com/2217/1831466417_657657428c_m.jpg "i-mode logo")According to Wikipedia: [**i-mode**](http://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/I-mode) is a set of technologies and protocols designed to allow browsing through mini-pages specifically designed for mobile devices such as [phones](http://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Celular "Cell Phone") or [PDAs](http://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/PDA "PDA"). A while back, I had a phone that was marketed specifically for this feature, the **TSM30** from Movistar. Despite also having a camera, an SD card slot, Java games, etc., it wasn’t a good phone because it frequently “froze,” its interface wasn’t very intuitive, and aesthetically it was quite ugly. Unlike in Japan, which is the birthplace of this technology, in Spain and in Europe in general, i-mode isn’t widely used. Given this, and after discovering that creating an i-Mode website was quite simple—since it’s based on simplified HTML—I built a portal where users could download Java apps and games from these devices (especially for the TSM30). To my surprise, even today, despite not having touched this portal in years, it still appears at the top of search results for “[i-Mode games](http://www.google.es/search?hl=es&newwindow=1&rlz=1B3GGGL_esES239ES239&q=imode+juegos&btnG=Buscar&meta= "Search for i-Mode on Google")" on Google Spain. **[http://imode.xarop.com](http://imode.xarop.com/ "iMode portal")**
